Elder Spiritist's Breastplate  
 

Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 17
DEX: +6 WIS: +9 AGI: +6 MANA: +80
Effect: Chloroplast (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 12.0) at Level 45
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Elder Spiritist's Breastplate
Lore Item
Slot: CHEST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 17
DEX: +6 WIS: +9 AGI: +6 MANA: +80
Effect: Chloroplast (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 12.0) at Level 45
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):10, 50, 10
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:750 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:15000
Submitted By:Kainazzo Icestrike
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-19 01:45:02
Page Updated:Tue Apr 23rd, 2024

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 60,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Old Sebilis
NPC Name
The Fabled Trakanon
Trakanon


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Old Sebilis


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

Post Comment
Nice item
QuoteReply
# Aug 16 2004 at 5:43 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766592959
55 posts
I keep this in inventory for when I solo, it's nice to have this running when taking on some casting mobs, plus it's free mana.
▲ Page top
..
QuoteReply
# Apr 29 2003 at 9:58 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759969079
51 posts
Any lvl may wear this, but the effect on it will not work until lvl 45, as with most armor from the Kunark era(i.e. Blood ember BP, etc etc.) Great twink item, but it would be better to lvl to around 50+ and go get u some elysian =)

Edited, Tue Apr 29 10:23:35 2003
▲ Page top
... overrated IMHO
QuoteReply
# Jan 04 2003 at 5:15 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591762334628
View User Forum
54 posts
Sure its a nice item but i wouldnt wear it... in raid groups i just group chloro and if u dont have that spell yet u prolly shouldnt be in the raid group to get the BP though buying it is another story... now on the price of 60K for this DAMN ull b waiting in the bazaar for a while buddy im sorry but id rather try and get like FT4 items for that amount ;p oh well yes clicky chloro would be nice but ill stick with what im wearing but if im in a guild raid where i know the ppl im raiding with ill prolly pass this up for other guildys (though usually by the time they get to this lvl raids i help em get better gear then this ;p)
just my 2 CP
▲ Page top
RE: ... overrated IMHO
QuoteReply
# Jun 26 2004 at 1:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594573640660
175 posts
I agree......for the price and level requirement for the effect this is moronic to me. My level 23 Druid will have Chloroplast by the time he can use this effect. The only reason to have a clicky version would be to free-up a Spell-slot for some other spell. Not worth the cost when he can get better BPs for same price or lower.
If it could be worn by other races/classes,especially Melees(Rogue/Warrior/Berserker) it would be awesome. They could solo like crazy :-) But SOE hates the idea of players soloing so they make it as hard as possible,forcing players to group. I don't mind grouping,IF I'm in the mood to do so,but I hate being forced to do so. Soloing should be easier. MMORPGs aren't just for socializing,but also to have FUN!
Bottom line: For the price,get something better. Maybe something with much higher AC and Stats/Resists. More than 2k for AC 17 with minor stats is ridiculous. And 10.0 Weight? OY! My Back!
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 30 2002 at 7:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OMG I want this for my lvl 53 druid!!! DROOL
Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2002 at 3:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254858370
5 posts
If I were to give this to my 17th lvl Druid (my first attempt at a magic class) would it disappear?

I was told that that would happen?
▲ Page top
RE: Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2003 at 8:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783940542
11 posts
If it says recommended level, you can still wear it, you just won't get the full effect of the stat boosts, ect. If it says required, and correct me if I'm wrong on this, is the point where you won't be able to wear it.

Rathan Kaiser
▲ Page top
RE: Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2002 at 5:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253812287
74 posts
It would not disappear and you would get the full extent of the stats(notice it has no recommened level). BUT you will not be able to use its triggered effect until lvl45. So its a nice bp for your druid, stat wise, but the effect doesnt do anything for you until lvl45.
▲ Page top
RE: Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2002 at 5:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253812287
74 posts
It would not disappear and you would get the full extent of the stats(notice it has no recommened level). BUT you will not be able to use its triggered effect until lvl45. So its a nice bp for your druid, stat wise, but the effect doesnt do anything for you until lvl45.
▲ Page top
RE: Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Oct 04 2002 at 11:50 AM Rating: Default
Diepen
179 posts
What I have heard is that you do not get the full effect of the item's ac and stat bonuses until the recommended level, and you can't use the clicky effect until you can cast it anyway.

I have also heard that you just cannot equip it.

It would be nice to dispel the rumors and find out for sure.
▲ Page top
RE: Newbie Type Question:
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2002 at 11:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759526382
3 posts
I bought the ES Bracer for the Share wolf for my lev 15 dru... Not realizing that I couldn't trigger the effect... Gaeaman (15 Dru)
Innoruk

3,705 posts
Umm, get a life. What kind of loser pays real money for game items. This is pitiful.
▲ Page top
RE: I need one
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2002 at 2:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775019710
5 posts
Well when you realize that you pay to play for the game it isn't so bad, at least for some people. It would take some people a while to get this (say a month) and if you jus pay cash for it you don't have to pay for the month to get it plus, you get the extra month's time to do other stuff
▲ Page top
RE: I need one
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2002 at 7:41 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
You pay to play the game, sure. so you can enjoy the challenge and fun of the game. If you just go buy a bunch of high level gear, that you did not earn, with real life cash, you miss most of the fun of getting the stuff. Plus you end up broke in RL. I pity anyone who uses real life cash to buy gear like this.

My char ran around in banded armor for 20+ levels, and now, at level 52, has over 100kpp in gear, all earned. And no, I'm not some hardcore player with no life outside EQ, I've just been playing for 18 months.

Now, here's my main point: I am very proud of my character, and his equipment, because everything on him was either looted from a mob I killed, or bought with plat I earned from things I looted or made, IN GAME, and sold.

And my money, instead of being spent on pixels, was used to buy REAL LIFE stuff.

Edited, Wed Oct 30 19:21:41 2002
▲ Page top

# Feb 07 2002 at 9:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767089005
View User Forum
212 posts
60k for this? your server must either be really hard up for one of these, or everyone must have pp all over the place.
This would be lucky to get 22k now.
Yes, I know it has Chloroplast attached to it, but does that warrant 60k? No...
If I had to choose paying 60k for this, or sitting around waiting for the leather tunic to drop in Kael, I would, um...wait around in Kael.

Or why not just fight in Umbral Plains and get the Elysian Tunic? Much better stats (just no effect)

Well.. i have the Elder spirits guantlets on my druid and i have to say that they look awesome! To anyone that has the full set i am forever jealous of you. The guantlets have the effect snare, and the BP has the effect Chloro, so does every piece have an effect?
▲ Page top
RE: Well..
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2002 at 5:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770176339
View User Forum
71 posts
I have the full set and choose not to wear them all. The suit is all dark heavy green. You can hardly see any detailing at all, no flair, no panache. So I wear eBoots and golden chitin bracers on one wrist and don my square helm for a touch of gold & silver to relieve the heaviness of the solid dark green suit.
▲ Page top
RE: Well..
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2002 at 8:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252813383
159 posts
yes each piee has an effect boots or helm have levitate arms have dod or drones of doom nice to keep some of the stuff in an EE bag or something for the effect but you have to have it on to use the effect so you have to change out when you want to use.. weight is pretty bad on these also but thats just my 2cp
▲ Page top
RE: Well..
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2002 at 3:05 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591764942820
49 posts
Effects to the best of my memory....

Helm = Super. Camo.
BP = Chloro....obviously
Legs = Skin Like Diamond
Boots = Lev
Arms = Drones of Doom
Gaunts = Snare
Bracer = Group Wolf Form?(not sure about that one but i think thats it)

and that oughta bout do it for the whole set..

AC is pretty good for dru armor...but no stats other than the BP and legs. so to have a full set would be nice...but to wear a full set...i think not. i only have the gaunts and arms...and at 51...the arms need to go in a bag somewhere sadly cause i dont have time to root rot something at 35 dmg a tick i believe it is? especially since in 2 levels ill be double dot'n for 2k+ per minute

but blah blah blah didnt mean to ramble...

Semaj Ecap 51dru
Cazic Thule
▲ Page top
RE: Well..
QuoteReply
# Nov 25 2003 at 11:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255563594
135 posts
I have the gauntlets, bracer, helm, and arms... and I enjoy root rotting. I mem drifting death and engulfing roots. I root, then click the snare from the gauntlets (insurance and it's mana free) then cast drifting death and stack on the DoD from the arms (total DoT damage of nearly 1k per minute)... re-apply spells at around 50% of the mob's health (or as needed for the mob).

The nice thing is that 2 of the 4 spells I'm using don't cost me any mana...

Now to get back on the topic of this BP... I think it would be great for cutting manna cost even more when soloing. when root rotting I don't take much damage (if any) but the free chloro would eliminate the need for mana to heal when the occasional stray smacks me.

so anyway, that's my worth

oh, and my druid is here

▲ Page top
Better than you think
QuoteReply
# Dec 15 2001 at 4:23 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591772280970
View User Forum
52 posts
Ok,here goes folks, im a druid, and ill tell ya what, id use this!! this item is so awesome, i dont even know where to begin, but ill start with this. I keep regen up all the time, it has saved me so much trouble with healing that you wouldnt even know, its a life/mana saver. secondly, i ALWAYS keep regen up in my EXP groups AND my RAID groups. it DOES make medding for that next heal seem to come a little bit later and seems to give me just that much more mana for emergencies, sure, i never see the effects it seems, i mean, theres no sudden jump in life, but, i know its there, when ive been ina group and never got above 10M wihout regen, and then i start using regenand suddenly im at 40M all the time, tell me that aint noticing something. and as for the guy that said ALL druids are power hungry/wanna be gods. dude, get a grip, this item is extremely awesome, id use it ALL the time, and i would be VERY happy doing it. My only hope is that one day ill be on a raid where this drops, and ill get one, (doubtful, but hey, dreaming never hurts :P) as for everyone else that has this item, CONGRATS!!! this thing is great. and if you are a druid that says he/she WONT use it, well, frankly, your mad, nuff said.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 27 2001 at 12:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if theres anyone who doesn't want ES stuff, I'll take it!
RE: Will take it!
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2002 at 1:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754608586
32 posts
Nice try, but folks wont be giving this away to anyone except friends or twinks. As our druids replace it with better stuff, it goes to their twinks, friends twinks, or to coffers to be sold for raid expenses. Funny to see things like this that were once considered the "best" and "uberest" are now only used for playing twinks in the off hours when not raiding. Will be interesting to see if anything is planned in the future to replace some of the NTOV drops or SoL drops. Some of the SoL drops we have so far have replaced some of the NTOV stuff... But not many yet.

Not worth it anymore.
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2001 at 11:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966056418
94 posts
This breastplate isn't worth it anymore. It's easier and cheaper to buy 3 black marbles and go on a Kael or Icewall raid and loot the tunic to quest for the Velious BP. But if you haven't upgraded to Velious, like everyone should have, this is the best alternative; though your chances of picking one up are slim to none.

# Feb 20 2002 at 12:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966158421
5 posts

Whats that supposed to mean "like everyone should have"? not everyone buys it when they want to. Most people wait until they get near 35 before they buy Velious. Even if i had Velious I probably wouldnt quest for a Velious BP any time soon, id much rather go for the ES BP

#Anonymous, Posted: Aug 08 2001 at 10:12 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) some dude was trying to sell this in ec no one bought it as far as i know becasue its proboly really expensive and and he was a druid. what i want he would want to sell that, dude its an item of trakanon, nuff said. SUre its not out of velious but that doesn't that kunark ubermob loots is any less awsome. you try to find a bp better than this, he was an idiot
to all who think this crap:
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2001 at 4:31 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361583644
6 posts
This is so far beyond the vermiculated tunic it's nuts, folks. 80 mana...do you people realize that is more mana than you could get from the 4 extra wisdom of verm tunic even at level 60? Beyond that, did it ever cross your minds that once you have 200 wisdom, which many druids do at level 50, wisdom is USELESS...but +mana never is?

And that's ignoring the effect that saves us 200 mana a cast...

And god forbid someone mention that ac and agi are what keeps us alive when things go bad...

sheesh.

- disgusted wanderer of norrath, solro server
▲ Page top
RE: to all who think this crap:
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2001 at 12:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595176725256
View User Forum
7 posts
Wisdom isnt useless.. wisdom is what makes druid have mana in the first place. The more wisdom you have.. the more mana. I see your point though.
▲ Page top
RE: to all who think this crap:
QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2001 at 9:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765685182
39 posts
Clearly you do not see the point. Adding more wisdom after 200 adds almost no mana. That's why the mana on this item makes it great.
▲ Page top
RE: to all who think this crap:
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2002 at 2:21 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Post luclin, the mana added by wisdom over 200 has increased. However, 80 mana is still better. From everything I have seen, the mana added when wis is over 200 is calculated by level/10. Better htan the 1 mana per wis that you used to get, but would still need 14 wis at lvl 60 before it was more than 80 mana.
▲ Page top
